observed: M-009 explicitly hands off inherited-unresolved tracking and a deterministic disappearance test.
The committed validator rejects missing dependencies and predecessor unresolved claims that are neither inherited nor explicitly resolved.
Five deterministic Node checks passed in an isolated runtime using the committed source logic.
inferred: Continuity lineage can now fail closed when successor records silently omit inherited open work.
proposed: Integrate this invariant into a repository gate that loads actual operations/queue-updates/M-*.json records.
superseded: Relying on prose handoffs or numeric queue order alone to preserve unresolved continuity work.
unresolved: The validator is not yet wired into CI or a full repository reducer.
Historical queue updates do not all expose normalized unresolved, unresolved_inherited, and resolved_claims fields.

privacy_boundary: Public repository metadata and abstract project-memory structure only. Raw private chat, health and pet-health material, credentials, personal identifiers, and unreviewed uploads were excluded.
trust_limit: Passing the invariant proves only that declared dependencies preserve or resolve declared unresolved claims; it does not prove the underlying claims are true or that undeclared dependencies were discovered.
